mnemonics: JPD: Just Picture Data. Visualize the joint probability table for multiple variables to understand their combined likelihood.
keyPoints: A joint probability distribution describes the probability of two or more random variables taking on specific values simultaneously.
For discrete variables, it is represented by a probability mass function (PMF) P(X=x, Y=y).
The sum of all joint probabilities must equal 1.
commonMistakes: Confusing joint probability P(A ∩ B) with conditional probability P(A|B).
Assuming independence between variables without verification, leading to incorrect calculations of joint probabilities.
